subject: english
source: research/10-skill-tree-blueprints.md §1.2 Branch E6; GL Assessment Verbal Cloze + Sentence-Completion formats.
note: 150 items across 15 levels. L1-L2 grammar (tense/agreement), L3-L7 semantic fill (tier1→tier2 advanced, L5 gate), L8-L10 multi-blank (2/2-linked/3, L10 gate), L11-L12 paragraph, L13-L14 register, L15 integrated passage (gate). Requires vocabulary L10. Unlocks reading L10+ (after L15).

items.prompt: Yesterday, the girl ___ to the shops with her mum.
Tomorrow, they ___ travel to the zoo on the train.
Last week, I ___ my grandmother for tea.
Right now, the baby ___ quietly in the cot.
Every morning, the postman ___ our letters early.
An hour ago, the train ___ the station in a hurry.
Next year, Sam ___ finish primary school and move up.
At the moment, she ___ her homework at the table.
While I was reading, the cat ___ on my warm lap.
Last summer, we ___ to the seaside for our holiday.
The children ___ laughing loudly at the funny clown.
Neither the cat nor the dog ___ hungry tonight.
Each of the students ___ a shiny new book.
My parents ___ watching the news downstairs.
Everyone in the class ___ finished the test early.
The news ___ on at six o'clock every evening.
A box of chocolates ___ on the kitchen table.
The team ___ won three matches this busy season.
Nobody ___ sure what happened after the bell rang.
Both of the twins ___ going to the birthday party.
The baby cried because her milk bottle was ___.
The sun is very ___ today, so wear your sunhat.
Please ___ the door before you leave the house.
Mum was very ___ when I dropped her favourite vase.
The ice cream was too ___ to eat right away.
We were ___ to be late again for school.
The bread was very ___, so we toasted it first.
The puppy is very ___ and wags its tail a lot.
Jane ran ___ down the hill to beat her brother.
The room was very ___; nobody had cleaned it yet.
The teacher asked the class to ___ the maths problem.
The author wanted to ___ the meaning clearly to readers.
Scientists ___ many experiments to test the new idea.
Her careful planning helped her to ___ her ambitious goal.
We must ___ the rules of the public library at all times.
The report will ___ the committee's key findings next week.
The stormy weather may ___ our weekend plans completely.
Her moving speech was designed to ___ the whole audience.
The long letter failed to ___ the reader's full attention.
We should ___ the evidence carefully before deciding.
The captain's ___ order silenced the noisy crew at once.
Her ___ manner put every nervous child at ease.
The latest discovery was ___; nobody had expected it.
The noise grew ___; it drowned out all our voices.
The ancient castle stood ___ on the windswept hilltop.
Her clever arguments were ___ and won the whole debate.
The puzzle proved ___; it took many hours to solve.
The weary climbers were ___ after the long mountain ascent.
The scientist's new theory was ___; top experts rejected it.
The politician's promises seemed ___, so many voters doubted.
The professor offered a ___ critique of the final essay.
Her ___ eyes scanned the dark room for any danger.
The accusation was ___, based on solid witness evidence.
The speaker made a ___ gesture toward the waiting audience.
The courtroom was ___, hanging on every whispered word.
The king's ___ command silenced the whole hall instantly.
The fog grew ___, blanketing the whole silent valley.
Her ___ reply truly surprised the careful interviewer.
The child's ___ laughter filled the warm sunny garden.
The minister's ___ response disappointed many reporters.
The novel's plot was ___, keeping readers guessing until the end.
His ___ criticism offended many of the quiet listeners.
The judge's final ruling was ___ and unquestionably fair.
The ___ storm caused widespread damage across the region.
Her ___ generosity inspired the whole local community.
The explorer's ___ journey took three long years to complete.
The ___ of the old building impressed every single visitor.
Her public ___ rose steadily as she worked ever harder.
The home team's ___ surprised all of their fierce rivals.
The minister issued a ___ apology to the whole parliament.
___ it rained heavily, the match ___ on schedule.
___ the bad traffic, we ___ on time for the film.
The library ___ quiet ___ nobody was revising at all.
The team ___ trained hard ___ won the trophy.
She ___ the violin beautifully ___ she was very tired.
The kettle ___ boiling ___ Tom forgot to switch it off.
We ___ at the station ___ the train was cancelled.
The baker ___ early ___ the bread would be fresh.
They ___ searching ___ they finally found the lost keys.
The dog ___ loudly ___ the postman opened the gate.
The harder she ___, the quicker she ___ the answer.
The more I ___, the less I ___ about the problem.
Not only ___ the rain stop, but the sun ___ too.
She ___ so softly that nobody ___ her warning.
If the alarm ___ earlier, we ___ have caught the bus.
Whenever the wind ___, the old gate ___ loudly.
The louder the thunder ___, the faster the children ___.
Had the referee ___ the foul, the striker ___ a penalty.
The colder it ___, the more tightly she ___ her coat.
No sooner ___ the match ended than the rain ___ down.
The ___ scientist ___ many notes ___ the careful experiment.
___ the rain stopped, the children ___ outside ___ play.
The ___ coach ___ the team ___ they had finally won.
She ___ the letter ___ her desk ___ she could find it later.
___ the wind ___ stronger, the boats ___ back to harbour.
The ___ dog ___ a stick ___ its excited young owner.
The cake ___ ready ___ the guests ___ at the door.
___ the bell rang, the pupils ___ their books ___ silence.
The ___ actor ___ the audience ___ a heartfelt bow.
___ the river rose, the villagers ___ sandbags ___ the banks.
The old lighthouse stood ___ on the cliff. Waves crashed ___ against the rocks. Sailors watched it ___ through the thick fog. It was a ___ sight. Without it, many ships ___ been lost at sea.

The old lighthouse stood proudly on the cliff. Waves crashed ___ against the rocks. Sailors watched it carefully through the thick fog. It was a welcome sight. Without it, many ships would have been lost at sea.

On market day the square was ___ with shoppers. Stalls sold fresh ___ from nearby farms. The baker's ___ smelled warm and sweet. A ___ cat watched from a wall. Above the noise, a bell ___ from the church tower.

On market day the square was bustling with shoppers. Stalls sold fresh ___ from nearby farms. The baker's bread smelled warm and sweet. A ginger cat watched from a wall. Above the noise, a bell chimed from the church tower.

The climbers reached the ___ as the sun rose. They were tired but ___ to have made it. Below them, the valleys were ___ in soft mist. A cold wind ___ their faces and made their eyes water. They knew the ___ down would be harder still.

The climbers reached the summit as the sun rose. They were tired but ___ to have made it. Below them, the valleys were wrapped in soft mist. A cold wind stung their faces and made their eyes water. They knew the descent down would be harder still.

The gardener worked ___ all summer long. She pulled up every stubborn ___ by the roots. The flower beds soon looked ___ and full of colour. Neighbours often stopped to ___ her careful work. By autumn the garden was the ___ on the whole street.

The gardener worked patiently all summer long. She pulled up every stubborn ___ by the roots. The flower beds soon looked tidy and full of colour. Neighbours often stopped to admire her careful work. By autumn the garden was the finest on the whole street.

The train was ___ the moment we reached the platform. People ___ onto the carriages, hugging bags. Guards blew ___ and raised their flags. We found our seats and ___ heavily into them. Outside, the grey city began to ___ past the window.

The train was leaving the moment we reached the platform. People ___ onto the carriages, hugging bags. Guards blew whistles and raised their flags. We found our seats and sank heavily into them. Outside, the grey city began to slip past the window.

The old library had not ___ a visitor for many weeks. Dust had ___ upon every shelf like snow. When Anna pushed open the ___ door, the hinges groaned. She stepped into the ___ silence and looked around. Shafts of light ___ through the high windows. Somewhere deep inside, a single page ___ down. She had finally found what she was ___ for.

The old library had not welcomed a visitor for many weeks. Dust had ___ upon every shelf like snow. When Anna pushed open the heavy door, the hinges groaned. She stepped into the echoing silence and looked around. Shafts of light slanted through the high windows. Somewhere deep inside, a single page drifted down. She had finally found what she was searching for.

Our team had trained every afternoon for the ___ match. The coach warned us that our rivals were ___. On the day, a bitter wind ___ across the cold pitch. Yet we played with such ___ that nobody seemed to notice. At half-time the score was still ___. In the second half, Asha ___ down the wing and fired a shot. It flew into the net and the crowd ___ wildly.

Our team had trained every afternoon for the crucial match. The coach warned us that our rivals were ___. On the day, a bitter wind whipped across the cold pitch. Yet we played with such determination that nobody seemed to notice. At half-time the score was still nil. In the second half, Asha raced down the wing and fired a shot. It flew into the net and the crowd cheered wildly.

The tiny seed had been ___ deep beneath the winter soil. For weeks the ground above was ___ with frost. Then, one morning in March, a thin green shoot ___ its way up. It grew a little each day, ___ by the rain. Bees that buzzed nearby ___ yellow pollen on its petals. By June it was a tall ___ of crimson colour. Passers-by often ___ to take a photograph.

The tiny seed had been buried deep beneath the winter soil. For weeks the ground above was ___ with frost. Then, one morning in March, a thin green shoot pushed its way up. It grew a little each day, nourished by the rain. Bees that buzzed nearby dusted yellow pollen on its petals. By June it was a tall flower of crimson colour. Passers-by often paused to take a photograph.

The museum was ___ a special new exhibition next week. Banners outside ___ the names of famous painters. Tourists queued ___ despite the pouring rain. Inside, the rooms were ___ in soft golden light. Each painting was ___ behind thick sheets of glass. A quiet guide ___ answered any questions in a whisper. By closing time the visitors ___ reluctant to leave.

The museum was opening a special new exhibition next week. Banners outside announced the names of famous painters. Tourists queued patiently despite the pouring rain. Inside, the rooms were bathed in soft golden light. Each painting was ___ behind thick sheets of glass. A quiet guide courteously answered any questions in a whisper. By closing time the visitors were reluctant to leave.

Long ago, a curious traveller set out to ___ an unknown continent. He packed his bags with maps and ___ supplies of food. For many months he sailed across a ___ ocean. Storms ___ his little ship, but he pressed on bravely. At last he saw a green coastline ___ on the horizon. Strange birds ___ overhead as he stepped onto the sand. He knew at once that he had ___ made history.

Long ago, a curious traveller set out to explore an unknown continent. He packed his bags with maps and generous supplies of food. For many months he sailed across a ___ ocean. Storms battered his little ship, but he pressed on bravely. At last he saw a green coastline looming on the horizon. Strange birds circled overhead as he stepped onto the sand. He knew at once that he had truly made history.
In a formal letter you should choose the word ___ over 'get'.
In a chatty text message you might write ___ instead of 'purchased'.
A school report would use ___ rather than 'kicked off'.
A friendly email to a mate would probably say ___ not 'converse'.
In a scientific essay prefer ___ instead of 'a load of'.
A casual chat with a friend would use ___ instead of 'inquired'.
A formal report should say ___ rather than 'messed up'.
Everyday talk between pals uses ___ not 'perspicacious'.
A business letter would prefer ___ instead of 'heaps of'.
A text to your sibling would use ___ not 'expeditiously'.
In an academic essay, the word ___ is preferred over 'big'.
A formal proposal should use ___ rather than 'help out'.
In parliamentary debate ___ is preferred over 'said loudly'.
A scientific paper uses ___ instead of 'found out'.
A legal contract prefers ___ instead of 'earlier'.
A historian's article would say ___ rather than 'happened'.
A diplomatic statement uses ___ instead of 'really upset'.
In a medical journal ___ is preferred over 'getting better'.
A minister's speech uses ___ rather than 'tell us'.
An academic abstract uses ___ rather than 'a lot of people'.

A sudden storm ___ the quiet coastal village. Fishing boats ___ inside the harbour walls for shelter. The lighthouse keeper ___ the heavy wooden shutters and braced the door. Rain ___ against the windows like tiny stones. Families gathered near their hearths and ___ in low voices. For hours the wind ___ outside, uprooting trees. When dawn arrived the village looked ___ but unbroken. Neighbours began the long task of ___ everything that had been damaged.

A sudden storm struck the quiet coastal village. Fishing boats ___ inside the harbour walls for shelter. The lighthouse keeper barred the heavy wooden shutters and braced the door. Rain hammered against the windows like tiny stones. Families gathered near their hearths and spoke in low voices. For hours the wind raged outside, uprooting trees. When dawn arrived the village looked battered but unbroken. Neighbours began the long task of repairing everything that had been damaged.

Deep in the ___ forest, a tiny stream began its long journey. It bubbled ___ over mossy stones and slipped between silent trees. Fish no larger than a finger ___ through the clear water. On the banks, ferns and ___ grew in every shade of green. Further downstream the waters gathered ___ and became a busy river. Boats began to appear, loaded with ___ and passengers. Towns rose along the banks, ___ on the river for trade. By the time it reached the sea, the stream had grown ___ beyond recognition.

Deep in the ancient forest, a tiny stream began its long journey. It bubbled cheerfully over mossy stones and slipped between silent trees. Fish no larger than a finger ___ through the clear water. On the banks, ferns and wildflowers grew in every shade of green. Further downstream the waters gathered speed and became a busy river. Boats began to appear, loaded with goods and passengers. Towns rose along the banks, depending on the river for trade. By the time it reached the sea, the stream had grown vastly beyond recognition.

Long ago, a curious inventor built a ___ machine in his attic. Neighbours gathered below and ___ at the strange noises. Steam and smoke ___ from every crack in the roof. Yet the inventor worked on, ___ by the doubts of others. After many years of ___ effort, a polished copper device stood finished. With a ___ turn of the key, the machine whirred into life. Within weeks, his invention had ___ the whole town. What had once seemed ___ now looked perfectly possible.

Long ago, a curious inventor built a peculiar machine in his attic. Neighbours gathered below and ___ at the strange noises. Steam and smoke drifted from every crack in the roof. Yet the inventor worked on, undeterred by the doubts of others. After many years of patient effort, a polished copper device stood finished. With a careful turn of the key, the machine whirred into life. Within weeks, his invention had transformed the whole town. What had once seemed impossible now looked perfectly possible.

Every morning, old Mr Patel ___ his garden with great care. He watered the roses, ___ the hedges, and fed the birds. The garden had become his ___ after many decades of devotion. Visitors often ___ at the bold colours and tidy paths. Children from the lane would ___ to watch the butterflies. On sunny afternoons the old man would ___ in his wicker chair. He knew that nothing could truly ___ the peace he had created. When he finally grew tired, he simply ___ the watering can to his granddaughter.

Every morning, old Mr Patel tended his garden with great care. He watered the roses, trimmed the hedges, and fed the birds. The garden had become his sanctuary after many decades of devotion. Visitors often ___ at the bold colours and tidy paths. Children from the lane would pause to watch the butterflies. On sunny afternoons the old man would doze in his wicker chair. He knew that nothing could truly disturb the peace he had created. When he finally grew tired, he simply passed the watering can to his granddaughter.

The expedition set off across the ___ tundra before sunrise. Sleds glided silently over ice that ___ in the pale blue light. Each traveller wore thick ___ to guard against the biting cold. For days they saw no living thing, only the ___ horizon ahead. At night they huddled around a tiny stove and ___ stories of home. A distant aurora ___ green and violet above the ice. By the end of the journey, their faces were ___ but their spirits were high. They had crossed where few had ever ___.

The expedition set off across the frozen tundra before sunrise. Sleds glided silently over ice that glittered in the pale blue light. Each traveller wore thick furs to guard against the biting cold. For days they saw no living thing, only the empty horizon ahead. At night they huddled around a tiny stove and ___ stories of home. A distant aurora shimmered green and violet above the ice. By the end of the journey, their faces were weathered but their spirits were high. They had crossed where few had ever ventured.
